records.title: Standard rent
records.description: The standard rent of any premises shall be the rent at which the premises were let on the 1st day of October 1987 or where the premises were not let on the said date, the rent at which they were last let before the said date; and where the premises were not let before the said date, the rent fixed by agreement between the landlord and tenant at the commencement of tenancy.
records.keywords: rent, premises, said, date, standard, before, october, last, fixed, agreement, between, landlord

records.act_name: Maharashtra Shops and Establishments (Regulation of Employment and Conditions of Service) Act, 2017
records.title: Hours of work
records.description: No employee shall be required or permitted to work in any establishment for more than nine hours in any day and forty-eight hours in any week. The employer shall not allow any employee to work for more than five hours consecutively without giving him a rest interval of at least half an hour.
records.keywords: hours, work, employee, more, required, permitted, establishment, nine, forty, eight, week, employer

records.act_name: Maharashtra Prevention of Dangerous Activities Act, 1981
records.title: Power to detain persons
records.description: The State Government may, if satisfied with respect to any person that with a view to preventing him from acting in any manner prejudicial to the maintenance of public order, it is necessary so to do, make an order directing that such person be detained.
records.keywords: person, power, detain, persons, satisfied, respect, view, preventing, acting, manner, prejudicial, maintenance

records.title: Abolition of proprietary rights
records.description: On and from the date of vesting, all rights, title and interests of the Zamindar in the estate and in the trees, standing crops, buildings, structures and other things attached to the land or permanently fastened to anything attached to the land, shall cease and be vested in the State Government.
records.keywords: rights, attached, land, abolition, proprietary, date, vesting, title, interests, zamindar, estate, trees

records.title: Landlord cannot recover possession except on grounds
records.description: No order or decree for the recovery of possession of any premises shall be made by any court in favour of the landlord against the tenant on any ground other than any of the grounds specified in section 27.
records.keywords: landlord, possession, grounds, cannot, recover, except, decree, recovery, premises, made, court, favour

records.act_name: Karnataka Shops and Commercial Establishments Act, 1961
records.title: Hours of work
records.description: Subject to the provisions of this Act, no adult employee shall be required or permitted to work in any establishment for more than nine hours on any day and for more than forty-eight hours in any week.
records.keywords: hours, work, more, subject, provisions, adult, employee, required, permitted, establishment, nine, forty

records.title: Restriction on transfer
records.description: Save as otherwise provided in this Act, no transfer of agricultural land held by a tenant shall be valid unless such transfer is made with the previous permission of the Deputy Commissioner.
records.keywords: transfer, restriction, save, otherwise, agricultural, land, held, tenant, valid, unless, made, previous

records.title: Powers of police to prevent commission of offences
records.description: The police may take such measures as they deem fit to prevent the commission of any cognizable offence and for the maintenance of public order and tranquility in any area within their jurisdiction.
records.keywords: police, prevent, commission, powers, offences, take, measures, deem, cognizable, offence, maintenance, public

records.act_name: Tamil Nadu Shops and Establishments Act, 1947
records.title: Hours of work in establishments
records.description: No person employed in an establishment shall be required or allowed to work therein for more than eight hours on any day and for more than forty-eight hours in any week: Provided that a person employed in an establishment may be required or allowed to work therein for more than eight hours in a day or forty-eight hours in a week if he is paid for the overtime work at twice the rate of his normal wages.
records.keywords: hours, work, eight, more, person, employed, establishment, required, allowed, therein, forty, week

records.title: Recovery of possession by landlord
records.description: Notwithstanding anything contained in any law or contract, no order or decree for the recovery of possession of any building shall be made by any court in favour of the landlord against the tenant except on one or more of the following grounds, namely, that the tenant has not paid or tendered the rent due.
records.keywords: recovery, possession, landlord, tenant, notwithstanding, anything, contained, contract, decree, building, made, court

records.title: Power to detain persons
records.description: The Government may, if satisfied with respect to any person that with a view to preventing him from acting in any manner prejudicial to the maintenance of public order or from smuggling or abetting the smuggling of any goods, it is necessary so to do, make an order directing that such person be detained.
records.keywords: person, smuggling, power, detain, persons, satisfied, respect, view, preventing, acting, manner, prejudicial

records.act_name: Kerala Buildings (Lease and Rent Control) Act, 1965
records.title: Eviction of tenants
records.description: A landlord who seeks to recover possession of a building from the tenant shall apply to the Rent Control Court having jurisdiction over the area in which the building is situated and the Rent Control Court shall if it is satisfied that the claim of the landlord falls within any of the categories specified in sub-section (3), pass an order directing the tenant to put the landlord in possession of the building.
records.keywords: landlord, possession, building, tenant, rent, control, court, eviction, tenants, seeks, recover, apply

records.title: Power to regulate traffic
records.description: The District Police Chief may, by general or special order, regulate the movement of traffic in any public place. Such regulation may include prohibiting or restricting traffic or pedestrians and may be made to include public safety, health, convenience, free passage and the prevention of crime.
records.keywords: traffic, regulate, public, include, power, district, police, chief, general, special, movement, place

records.act_name: Gujarat Tenancy and Agricultural Lands Act, 1948
records.title: Right of permanent tenure of tenant
records.description: Every tenant in possession of land on or after the appointed day shall, subject to the provisions of this Act, be entitled to hold such land as a permanent tenant; and no landlord shall be entitled to recover possession of the land from such tenant.
records.keywords: tenant, land, permanent, possession, entitled, right, tenure, every, after, appointed, subject, provisions

records.act_name: Gujarat Shops and Establishments (Regulation of Employment and Conditions of Service) Act, 2019
records.title: Restriction on working hours
records.description: No employee shall be required or permitted to work in any establishment for more than nine hours in any day and forty-eight hours in any week. The State Government may, by notification, extend the hours of work in any establishment for specific occasions.
records.keywords: hours, work, establishment, restriction, working, employee, required, permitted, more, nine, forty, eight

records.act_name: Uttar Pradesh Zamindari Abolition and Land Reforms Act, 1950
records.title: Bhumidhari with non-transferable rights
records.description: A person who is or is deemed to be a Bhumidhar with non-transferable rights, shall hold his holding subject to the condition that he shall not, so long as he holds such holding, transfer the same by sale, gift, mortgage with possession, exchange or otherwise.
records.keywords: transferable, rights, holding, bhumidhari, person, deemed, bhumidhar, hold, subject, condition, long, holds

records.act_name: Uttar Pradesh Gangsters and Anti-Social Activities (Prevention) Act, 1986
records.title: Attachment of property
records.description: Where the State Government is satisfied that a person is a gangster, it may direct the District Magistrate concerned to take steps for the attachment of the property, movable or immovable, acquired by such gangster from the proceeds of the gangster activities.
records.keywords: gangster, attachment, property, satisfied, person, direct, district, magistrate, concerned, take, steps, movable

records.title: Protection of tenant against eviction
records.description: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in any other law or any contract, no order or decree for the recovery of possession of any premises shall be made by any court in favour of the landlord against a tenant except on one or more of the following grounds, namely, that the tenant has neither paid nor tendered the rent due.
records.keywords: tenant, against, protection, eviction, notwithstanding, anything, contrary, contained, other, contract, decree, recovery

records.title: Power to issue prohibitory orders
records.description: Whenever the Commissioner of Police considers it necessary so to do for the preservation of the public peace or public safety or for the prevention of riots or affrays, or for preventing obstruction to traffic, he may make an order in writing calling on any person or persons, or the public generally, to abstain from any act, or to take certain order with respect to certain matter or thing, for the period to be specified in the order.
records.keywords: public, certain, power, issue, prohibitory, orders, whenever, commissioner, police, considers, necessary, preservation

records.title: Right of tenant to sub-let
records.description: A tenant shall not sub-let the whole or any part of the premises let to him or transfer or assign his interest therein without the previous consent in writing of the landlord. The landlord shall not withhold his consent unreasonably.
records.keywords: tenant, consent, landlord, right, whole, part, premises, transfer, assign, interest, therein, without

records.act_name: West Bengal Security of Land Tenure Act, 1955
records.title: Definition of bargadar
records.description: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires, 'bargadar' means a person who under the system generally known as adhi, barga or bhag cultivates the land of another person on condition of delivering a share of the produce of such land to that person.
records.keywords: person, bargadar, land, definition, unless, context, otherwise, requires, means, system, generally, known

records.title: Registration of establishment
records.description: Every employer shall, before or within thirty days of the establishment becoming operative or coming into existence, as the case may be, make an application to the Inspector having jurisdiction for the registration of his establishment.
records.keywords: establishment, registration, every, employer, before, within, thirty, days, becoming, operative, coming, existence

records.title: Prohibition of import, etc. of intoxicating liquor
records.description: No person shall import, export, transport, manufacture, sell, buy, possess, use or consume any intoxicating liquor or hemp in the State of Telangana except under and in conformity with the conditions of a licence, permit, pass or authorization granted under this Act.
records.keywords: import, intoxicating, liquor, prohibition, person, export, transport, manufacture, sell, possess, consume, hemp

records.title: Rights of Khatedar tenant
records.description: Subject to the provisions of this Act, a Khatedar tenant shall have a heritable and transferable right of occupancy in the land held by him and shall be entitled to use the land for any purpose other than non-agricultural purpose.
records.keywords: khatedar, tenant, land, purpose, rights, subject, provisions, heritable, transferable, right, occupancy, held
